name: Pork Chops in Wine Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the sage, rosemary, garlic, salt, and pepper.
2. Press a little of this mixture firmly into both sides of each pork chop.
3. Melt butter and olive oil in a heavy 10 to 12 inch skillet.
4. Brown the chops on both sides, turning carefully with tongs.
5. Remove the chops and pour off all but a small amount of fat from the pan.
6. Add 2/3 of the wine and bring to boil.
7. Return the chops to the pan. Cover, reduce heat, and simmer until the chops are tender when pressed with the tip of a knife, about 25 to 30 minutes.
8. When ready to serve, remove the chops to a heated plate.
9. Add the remaining wine to the skillet and boil down to a syrupy glaze.
10. Pour the glaze over the chops.
